name: Aralar Tour
name_original: Sarastarri desde el puerto De Lizarrusti — trailrunning por el Parque Natural de Aralar
description: This difficult 6.7-mile (10.9 km) trail running route from Lizarrusti to Sarastarri in Aralar Natural Park features 1400 feet (427 metres) o
region_intro: Jogging routes around Ataun traverse a diverse landscape within the Goierri region of Gipuzkoa, Spain, with a significant portion lying within the Aralar Natural Park. The area features mountainous orography, with elevations ranging from 180 to nearly 1,000 meters, offering varied terrain from gentle riverside paths along the Agauntza River to challenging ascents. Extensive forest areas, open grasslands, and unique limestone formations like the Ataun Dome characterize the natural environment, providing a rich setting for running. Best jogging routes around Ataun The most popular jogging route is Aralar Tour, a 6.7 miles (10.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation changes within the Aralar Natural Park. Another top favourite among local runners is Cascada de Osinberde por el sendero de Osinberde (SL-Gi 2009) — trailrunning por el Parque Natural de Aralar, a moderate 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This route leads through the natural park, offering scenic views and a waterfall. Local runners also love the Lareo Reservoir – Lareo Reservoir loop from Lizarrustiko lepoa, an 11.2 miles (17.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and past the Lareo Reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Jogging around Ataun is defined by mountainous terrain, extensive forest areas, and the Agauntza River, with a network of trails suitable for various abilities. The region offers options from moderate paths to difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Ataun are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Nearly 3,000 runners have used komoot to explore Ataun's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Ataun?
A: Ataun offers a wide selection of jogging routes, with over 200 trails available. These routes vary significantly in difficulty, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes around Ataun?
A: Jogging routes in Ataun feature diverse terrain, ranging from gentle riverside paths along the Agauntza River to challenging ascents within the Aralar Natural Park. You'll encounter mountainous landscapes, extensive beech forests, open grasslands, and unique limestone formations like the Ataun Dome.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Ataun?
A: While many routes in Ataun are classified as moderate to difficult due to the mountainous terrain and elevation changes, there are a few easier options available. For instance, you can find some less strenuous paths along the Agauntza River or explore sections of the natural park that are flatter.

Q: What natural features can I discover while running in Ataun?
A: Ataun's running routes are rich in natural beauty. You can explore the vast Aralar Natural Park with its high-mountain areas and extensive forests, follow the scenic Agauntza River, or witness the unique limestone formations of the Ataun Dome. Many routes also offer panoramic views of the Aralar summits.

Q: Are there any jogging routes that feature waterfalls in Ataun?
A: Yes, several running routes in Ataun pass by beautiful waterfalls. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058676642/cascada-de-osinberde-por-el-sendero-de-osinberde-sl-gi-2009-trailrunning-por-el-parque-natural-de-aralar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Osinberde por el sendero de Osinberde&lt;/a&gt; leads directly to a waterfall. You can also find other waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3049380" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amezketa Stream Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553270" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osinberde Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; along various trails.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Ataun?
A: Absolutely. Many of Ataun's jogging routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A notable example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20519225" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lareo Reservoir – Lareo Reservoir loop from Lizarrustiko lepoa&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through mountainous terrain and past the Lareo Reservoir.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Ataun?
A: The running routes in Ataun are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural landscapes within the Aralar Natural Park, and the variety of routes that offer both physical challenges and breathtaking views.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest along the jogging routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can encounter several points of interest. The Lizarrusti Interpretation Centre, a main entrance to the Aralar Natural Park, serves as a starting point for many routes. You might also pass by historical buildings in Ataun or discover unique geological formations like the Ataun Dome, which features over 200 caves, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3890053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puttarri Cave Passage&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for jogging trails in Ataun?
A: Many routes, especially those entering the Aralar Natural Park, have designated parking areas. Lizarrusti, a key entrance to the natural park, is a well-known starting point for numerous trails and typically offers parking facilities.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Ataun?
A: Ataun offers great jogging opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, but higher elevation routes offer cooler conditions. Winter jogging is possible, especially on lower trails, but be prepared for colder weather and potentially snowy conditions in the mountains.

Q: Are there any long-distance running paths that pass through Ataun?
A: Yes, Ataun is situated in a region rich with established trails. The GR-283 circuit, known as "The Idiazabal Cheese Trail," starts from the Lizarrusti Interpretation Centre. Other long-distance paths in the wider area include the Euskal Herria footpath (GR 12) and the Tour of Aralar (GR 20), offering extensive options for longer runs.

Q: Can I find amenities like cafes or pubs near the jogging trails in Ataun?
A: While Ataun is a rural area, you can find amenities within the town itself. The Lizarrusti Interpretation Centre, a common starting point for many routes, may also offer some facilities. For more options, exploring the main areas of Ataun will provide access to local cafes and pubs.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Sarastarri trail running route?
faqs.answer: This trail is designed for trail running and typically takes around 1 hour and 40 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and are there parking options?
faqs.answer: The trail begins at the Port of Lizarrusti, which is a well-known access point to the Aralar Natural Park. There is often an interpretation center and an inn at Lizarrusti, suggesting amenities and parking facilities are available there for visitors.

faqs.question: Is the Sarastarri trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as difficult for trail running. It involves a significant elevation gain of over 400 meters and can be challenging, requiring good physical fitness and careful footing, especially in certain sections. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable features. You'll pass by the tranquil Lareo Reservoir, surrounded by beautiful beech forests. The trail also leads to the Monte Cormor (995 m) summit, and the area is known for the unique Sarastarri Cave and the Sarastarri Summit, which provides excellent panoramic views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, featuring mostly accessible paths but with some sections that demand careful footing. You'll navigate through extensive beech forests, and parts of the path can narrow into small tracks, particularly around the Lareo reservoir. Expect significant ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to trail run the Sarastarri route?
faqs.answer: The trail is beautiful year-round, but autumn is particularly vibrant when the beech trees around the Lareo reservoir are at their peak. Be aware that the area is prone to fog and low clouds, which can impact visibility, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for the Aralar Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Aralar Natural Park for activities like trail running. However, it's always a good idea to check with the Lizarrusti Interpretation Center for any current regulations or local advisories before your visit.

faqs.question: Is the Sarastarri trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many natural parks in Spain allow dogs, specific rules can vary. It's best to confirm with the Lizarrusti Interpretation Center or local park authorities regarding dog policies, including leash requirements, to ensure a smooth visit with your canine companion.

faqs.question: Are there any unique natural features to look out for?
faqs.answer: Yes, beyond the stunning beech forests and the Lareo Reservoir, the area is notable for the Sarastarri Cave, a unique karst cave with a small internal lagoon. If you plan to explore it, a headlamp is essential. The region around the Sarastarri summit also features numerous other caves.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the Aralar Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail running route intersects with several other official paths. A significant portion of the route follows sections of the Aralarko bira (72%) and the Lareo ibilbidea (69%). You'll also find it crosses parts of the Artzaintza naturbidea (54%) and the E1 Gaztaren Ibilbidea: Lizarrusti - Ordizia (53%), among others.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from the Sarastarri summit?
faqs.answer: From the Sarastarri summit, you can expect privileged panoramic views of the Aralar region. On clear days, you'll be able to see prominent peaks like Txindoki and Ganboa, and even the distant Urbasa and Andía mountain ranges, offering expansive vistas of the surrounding natural landscape.
